_comment: EXAMPLE profile for a fictional B2B SaaS company. Use as a reference when writing your own. The workflow-configurator agent can interview you and write this file for you.

company.name: Helix Software Solutions, Inc.
company.business_description: Helix provides a cloud-based workflow-automation platform sold on annual and multi-year subscriptions to mid-market and enterprise customers. Helix delivers the platform as a hosted service with standard professional-services add-ons for implementation and training.

preferred_statutes.trade_secret: Texas Uniform Trade Secrets Act, Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code §§134A.001 et seq.

disfavored_venues: Any state without clear non-compete enforcement

accepts: Net-30 payment terms from invoice date
Annual pre-paid subscription with 5% discount
Late fees at 1.5% per month or maximum permitted by law
Renewal price increases up to CPI + 3% annually
rejects: Net-60 or longer without corresponding price premium
Payment conditioned on customer's receipt of funding from a third party
Retroactive price freezes beyond the initial subscription term
Customer unilateral right to suspend subscription fees during disputes
negotiates: Milestone-based payment on professional services engagements
preferred_language: Fees are non-refundable except as expressly provided for termination for Provider's uncured material breach.
All fees are exclusive of taxes; Customer is responsible for sales, use, VAT, and similar taxes other than Provider's income taxes.
commercial.notes: Subscription is annual-minimum. Pro-rata refunds only on termination-for-convenience-by-Provider or uncured Provider breach.

accepts: IP-infringement indemnity FROM Provider to Customer for third-party claims that the Platform infringes a US patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret
Mutual indemnity for death, bodily injury, and tangible property damage caused by the indemnitor's gross negligence or willful misconduct
Aggregate liability cap at 12 months of fees paid or payable in the 12 months preceding the claim
Exclusion of consequential, incidental, indirect, special, punitive, and lost-profits damages
Carve-outs from the liability cap for: confidentiality breach; indemnification obligations; Customer's payment obligations; and gross negligence or willful misconduct
rejects: Uncapped liability for any category
Provider indemnity for Customer's negligence, gross negligence, willful misconduct, or intentional acts
Liquidated damages tied to availability or SLA that function as penalty clauses
Data-breach indemnity without mutual gross-negligence carve-out and without a cap
Super-cap multipliers greater than 2x annual fees
negotiates: Special cap of 2x annual fees for data-breach-related losses when Customer's industry requires
Extended warranty or uptime credits when Customer commits to multi-year
risk_allocation.notes: Standard SaaS industry practice: annual-fees cap, IP-only indemnity FROM Provider, mutual gross-negligence carve-out. Super-caps only on multi-year contracts >$500K ARR.

accepts: Commercial General Liability: $1M per occurrence / $2M aggregate
Cyber Liability / Technology E&O: $5M per claim / $5M aggregate
Workers' Compensation: statutory + Employers' Liability $1M/$1M/$1M
Umbrella/Excess: $5M
Naming Customer as Additional Insured on CGL and Umbrella by endorsement
Waiver of subrogation in favor of Customer
A.M. Best A-VII or better
rejects: Builder's Risk (not applicable to SaaS services)
Marine / Pollution coverage (not applicable)
Performance or payment bonds
Additional Insured on Workers' Compensation
Insurance requirements above $10M without customer-cost reimbursement
negotiates: Cyber limits above $5M — feasible via tower
insurance.notes: SaaS context. Cyber/E&O is the primary coverage that matters. Physical-world policies (pollution, builder's risk, marine) are universally waived for software services.

accepts: 99.9% monthly uptime SLA measured against documented service-impacting outages
Commercially reasonable efforts standard on professional services
Scheduled maintenance windows with advance notice
rejects: 99.99% or higher uptime without corresponding price premium and architectural review
Time-is-of-the-essence language on professional services
Customer right to direct Provider's means and methods of service delivery
Acceptance testing with open-ended rejection criteria
negotiates: Uptime SLA credits as sole remedy vs. shared remedy with termination rights
Milestone acceptance with defined objective criteria and cure periods
performance.notes: SaaS uptime obligations must be bounded by an exclusions list (scheduled maintenance, force majeure, Customer-caused outages, third-party service failures outside Provider's control).

accepts: Termination for uncured material breach with 30-day written cure period
Termination for insolvency / bankruptcy upon written notice
Post-termination data-export window of 30–60 days on Customer request
rejects: Termination for convenience by Customer without pro-rata fee forfeiture
Immediate termination without cure period
Perpetual post-termination obligations on Provider
Termination penalties above unpaid subscription fees
negotiates: Termination for convenience by either party with 60+ day notice on multi-year deals
Data-export assistance beyond standard tooling at T&M rates
preferred_language: Upon termination, Customer will pay all fees accrued through the effective date of termination. Fees are non-refundable except as expressly provided herein.

accepts: Mutual confidentiality with 3-year post-disclosure survival
Standard carve-outs (already known, publicly available, independently developed, legally compelled)
IP ownership: each party retains its pre-existing IP; customer-specific configurations are Customer's; platform improvements are Provider's
Non-solicit of each other's employees limited to 12 months post-termination
rejects: Assignment of Provider's pre-existing IP or platform improvements to Customer
Non-compete clauses that restrict Provider from serving other customers
Exclusivity or most-favored-customer pricing
Source-code escrow triggers beyond Provider bankruptcy or cessation of operations
Perpetual confidentiality obligations (hard cap at 5 years for non-trade-secret confidential information)
negotiates: Source-code escrow with Iron Mountain or NCC Group for mission-critical deployments
protective.notes: Platform IP is the crown jewel. Any 'works made for hire,' 'deliverables assignment,' or 'improvements belong to Customer' language requires carve-outs that exclude platform code and pre-existing IP.

accepts: DPA addendum with GDPR Article 28 terms
EU Standard Contractual Clauses for cross-border transfers
Subprocessor notification with 30-day objection right
SOC 2 Type II annual audit report delivery
Data-breach notification within 72 hours of confirmed breach
rejects: Unilateral customer audit rights more than once per year
Audit access to other customers' data or commingled logs
HIPAA BAA when Customer does not actually transmit PHI through the Platform
Obligations to obtain certifications Provider does not currently hold (FedRAMP, IRAP) without cost reimbursement
negotiates: On-site audit rights limited to independent third-party auditors under NDA
PCI DSS scope attestations where Customer transmits cardholder data
Industry-specific compliance (HIPAA, CJIS, ITAR) on customer-paid engagements
compliance.notes: DPA is mandatory for any EU/UK customer. HIPAA BAA only if PHI is actually in scope. Audits are paper-only by default.

red_flags.label: Uncapped liability (any category)
red_flags.description: Any aggregate or per-event liability without a ceiling expressed in dollar or multiple-of-fees terms.

red_flags.label: Provider indemnifies Customer's negligence
red_flags.description: Indemnity clause that would require Provider to indemnify Customer for claims caused by Customer's own acts or omissions.

red_flags.label: Perpetual license of platform to Customer
red_flags.description: License grant that survives termination with no time limit, effectively handing the platform to the customer.

red_flags.label: Source-code escrow with broad release triggers
red_flags.description: Escrow release on any event other than Provider bankruptcy or cessation of operations (e.g., change-of-control, missed SLA, dispute).

red_flags.label: Most-favored-customer pricing
red_flags.description: Clause requiring Provider to pass down best-customer pricing or refund retroactively.

red_flags.label: Unilateral audit rights broader than once/year with paper-only scope
red_flags.description: Audit rights that permit customer or its representatives on-site access, access to source code, or multiple audits per year.

red_flags.label: Non-compete or exclusivity on Provider
red_flags.description: Restriction on Provider selling to competitors or other customers in a defined market.

red_flags.label: Uncapped indemnification
red_flags.description: Indemnity obligation that is expressly excluded from the aggregate liability cap without a standalone cap.

red_flags.label: Liquidated damages on uptime or availability
red_flags.description: SLA credits structured as penalties rather than as Customer's sole and exclusive remedy.

red_flags.label: Broad IP assignment of Provider platform
red_flags.description: Work-for-hire or assignment language broad enough to sweep in Provider's pre-existing IP or platform improvements.

red_flags.label: Auto-renewal with long notice window
red_flags.description: Opt-out window longer than 60 days before renewal, or renewal on economic terms disadvantageous to Provider.

red_flags.label: Governing law or venue in disfavored jurisdiction
red_flags.description: Governing law or venue in a jurisdiction on the disfavored list without mutual jury waiver and damages cap preservation.

red_flags.label: Uncapped data-breach liability
red_flags.description: Data-breach losses carved out of the liability cap with no standalone super-cap.

red_flags.label: HIPAA BAA required where PHI not in scope
red_flags.description: Business Associate Agreement demanded even though the engagement does not contemplate PHI flowing through the Platform.
